items.title: Heat Worries US Wheat Farmers Just as Crop Prices Are Surging
items.description: For farmers in North Dakota surveying their wheat as harvest closes in, scorching heat is threatening yields just as crop prices rise to a three-year high.

items.title: Magnificent 7 Loses $767 Billion as AI Skeptics Dump Tech Stocks
items.description: The Magnificent Seven group of megacap technology stocks is on track for its biggest one-day drop since the tariff tantrum in April 2025 as results from Alphabet Inc. and Tesla Inc. are casting doubt

items.title: Kalshi Eyes Copper Perpetual Futures After Precious Metals Push
items.description: Kalshi Inc. is eyeing plans to launch perpetual copper futures that would offer investors a new way to bet on the red metal as the artificial intelligence buildout boosts demand.

items.title: BlackRock’s GIP Eyes Bundling Fund Stakes in Securitization Deal
items.description: BlackRock Inc.’s Global Infrastructure Partners LP has been in discussions with investors to sell bonds backed by stakes in some of its private capital funds, joining a growing list of asset managers

items.title: NYC’s Former HSBC Tower Seeks More Than $800 Million in Sale
items.description: A Manhattan office tower where Amazon.com Inc. has offices is going up for sale.

items.title: Chocolate Maker Lindt Faces US Lawsuit Over Child Labor Allegations

items.title: How Leveraged Exchange-Traded Funds Are Suddenly Everywhere
items.description: On this episode of Trillions, we discuss what’s fueling the boom and how these exchange-traded funds work in today’s market.

items.title: The Worrying Signal Embedded in the Momentum Selloff
items.description: A loss of market leadership.

items.title: Ireland Says Alumina Probe Finds No Proof of Russia Weapons Link
items.description: An investigation into alumina exports from Ireland to Russia has not found conclusive evidence linking a production plant to the manufacture of weapons in Russia, Irish Prime Minister Micheal Martin s

items.title: Blackstone Leads $400 Million Private Loan For HVAC Firm Buyout
items.description: Blackstone Inc. led a roughly $400 million deal to support the buyout of Integra Testing Services by Harvest Partners LP, according to people familiar with the matter.

items.title: Dollar Jumps on Haven Flows as Middle East War Intensifies
items.description: The dollar got a boost Thursday from haven flows as the war in the Middle East intensified, pushing oil prices above $100 a barrel.

items.title: Russia’s Black Sea Waters Not Safe for Ships Amid Attacks
items.description: Moscow warned that it is unsafe to navigate in its Black Sea waters after Ukraine has been ramping up attacks on Russia-linked ships in the area.

items.title: Dow Says Iran War Could Boost Guidance as Supply Chain Shielded

items.title: Nasdaq 100 Eyes Worst Day in Month as Oil Prices Soar Over $100
items.description: The Nasdaq 100 Index tumbled, putting it on track for its worst day in a month, as doubts about the payoffs from debt-fueled artificial-intelligence spending boom dragged down big tech stocks. Oil pri

items.title: Bund Yields Hit 15-Year High as $100 Oil Fans Inflation Concerns
items.description: German bond yields rose to the highest level since 2011 as surging oil prices prompted bigger bets on interest-rate hikes, even as the European Central Bank kept its benchmark unchanged.

items.title: Teck Second-Quarter Earnings Triple as Copper Prices Advance
items.description: Canadian miner Teck Resources Ltd. reported a tripling of second-quarter earnings on higher copper prices, while reaffirming output guidance, including at its flagship Quebrada Blanca mine in Chile.

items.title: Gold Falls as Middle East Conflict Reverses Dip-Buying Momentum
items.description: Gold fell as escalating conflict in the Middle East continued to push up energy prices, fueling bets the Federal Reserve will tighten monetary policy to contain inflation.

items.title: Stocks Hit by AI and War Jitters as Oil Tops $100: Markets Wrap
items.description: A surge in oil spurred by the escalating Iran war sent stocks and bonds lower, with Wall Street also rattled by renewed concerns over whether massive artificial-intelligence investments will pay off.
